title: Bridge Essay: Neural correlates of metacognition in education: a machine learning approach.
concept: Metacognition as the 'diagnostic' capacity — science of knowing what you know, parallel to esoteric self-examination.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Metacognition as the 'diagnostic' capacity — science of knowing what you know, parallel to esoteric self-examination.

**Neural correlates of metacognition in education: a machine learning approach.** - Neuropsychologia (2025). DOI: 10.1016/j.neuropsychologia.2025.109265. PMID: 40885485. Metacognition, the ability to reflect and regulate one's cognitive processes, has been shown to play a role in various aspects of life, particularly in academic settings. While important steps have been made in uncovering the neural basis of metacognition for highly specific domains (such as perceptual and mnemonic decision-making), little is known about how these findings relate to general forms of metacognition relevant in education. In this study, we use a data-driven approach to (i) identify
